articles.description: The image captures the glowing ribbon of the galactic plane, where the vast majority of the Milky Way's stars are concentrated.

articles.title: A lunar base or a lunar economy?
articles.description: We applaud the lunar base vision laid out by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman on March 24. Placing this stake in the lunar regolith is long overdue. articles.title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts capture space history — on their iPhones
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts are capturing spectacular photos of the moon and Earth on their iPhones in space.

articles.title: Artemis 2 science gets underway as Orion begins its return trip

articles.title: 'It just made it even more special': Being so far from Earth makes you appreciate our planet even more, Artemis 2 astronaut says
articles.description: Seeing Earth from space changes your perspective. And this "overview effect" is magnified when you're looking back from beyond the moon, according to Artemis 2's Christina Koch.

articles.title: Earthset
articles.description: The Artemis II crew captured this view of Earth setting on April 6, 2026, as they flew around the Moon. articles.title: 'Unreal' solar eclipse: Artemis 2 crew just saw one of the rarest sights in spaceflight history

articles.title: NASA’s new moon base project requires operational technology systems in space, but they are vulnerable.

articles.title: The Artemis 2 astronauts just flew by the moon. Lunar scientists can't wait for what's next
articles.description: "Apollo happened before I was born, and the idea that we will see humans on the moon within our lifetimes, with decades of advances in science and technology behind them, is remarkable."

articles.title: Moon milestones: A rundown of Artemis 2's many spaceflight firsts
articles.description: You've probably heard it by now: NASA’s Artemis 2 mission was the first to launch astronauts to fly by the moon in more than 50 years. But that was just the first of the firsts for this mission.

articles.title: Artemis 2 breaks humanity's all-time distance record during historic loop around the moon (video)
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts have now traveled farther from Earth than any people in history, breaking the mark set by the Apollo 13 crew in April 1970.

articles.title: The moon up close: How the Artemis 2 astronauts are photographing their historic lunar flyby

articles.title: Astronaut Victor Glover is the latest in a long line of Black American explorers − including York, the enslaved man who played a key role in the Lewis and Clark expedition
